directions.name: Re-land the peeled hot kernel and trim the c_shared export path
directions.hypothesis: Round 4 is the strongest evidence for the final round: the peeled fixed-shape hot kernel reached 37.373951 ms, only about 0.088 ms behind the accepted 37.285807 ms base, while improving tensor active from 32.61% to 34.58% and lowering barrier stall from 8.93% to 6.54%. The remaining gap now looks memory-side rather than control-flow-side: DRAM throughput rose to 50.65%, short scoreboard to 14.29%, and LSU requests to about 82.77%. The best one-round move on the restored base is therefore to re-land the peeled hot-path structure and pair it with a cheaper epilogue/export path so the win from the peeled steady-state loop is not handed back in the shared-to-global writeout.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Epilogue-side shared/export traffic after the peeled steady-state loop improves control overhead.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:421-450 (current hot-loop region that the round-4 peeled specialization improved and would need to be re-landed)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:41-46 and src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:454-485 (per-warp c_shared scratch and shared export path to trim)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:517-550 (fixed-shape dispatch path that should select the peeled hot kernel plus unchanged 64x96 tail)
directions.risk: This combines two moving pieces, so the implementation can regress if the peeled hot path is not re-landed cleanly or if the export simplification introduces layout/correctness bugs.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if runtime does not beat the accepted base after re-landing the peeled hot path, or if epilogue changes fail to lower LSU / short-scoreboard pressure.

directions.name: Re-land the peeled hot kernel and deepen single-skew cp.async overlap
directions.hypothesis: The round-4 near-hit shows that fixed-shape steady-state peeling is the right base direction, but it also exposed a larger latency signal: short-scoreboard rose from 4.16% on the accepted base to 14.29%, even while barrier stall fell. That points to the next complementary move being a bounded prefetch/wait retune inside the existing single-skew layout rather than another producer/consumer rewrite. Re-land the peeled hot kernel, then adjust prefill depth, commit timing, or wait placement so the peeled loop keeps its barrier win while hiding more of the remaining copy latency.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Copy-latency / short-scoreboard exposure in the peeled steady-state loop.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:213-239 (existing single-skew A/B staging helpers that should remain the only feed path)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:415-450 (prefill, next-stage issue, and wait/barrier sequence inside the hot loop)
directions.risk: The overlap window is easy to get wrong, and moving waits or deepening prefetch can erase the peeled-hot-kernel gain or complicate correctness without much upside.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if short-scoreboard does not fall on top of the peeled hot path or if the retune raises registers/thread and regresses runtime.

directions.name: Re-land the peeled hot kernel with a bounded single-skew B stride retune
directions.hypothesis: Broad B-feed rewrites are still negative evidence: round 1 two-level B staging regressed badly, and round 3 warp-specialized staging improved one symptom but stayed much slower than the base. The only feed-side follow-up still justified for the final round is a narrowly bounded one: keep the current row-major single-skew semantics and re-land the peeled hot path, then test whether a modest stride/skew adjustment can reduce the remaining DRAM / LSU pressure that the round-4 run exposed without reopening the failed producer/consumer families.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual B-side staging inefficiency in the current single-skew layout, not a full feed-path redesign.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:47-52 (current single-skew B shared stride and tile sizing)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:208-239 (logical-to-shared B mapping and staging path to keep bounded)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:437-445 (warp-side B fragment loads from the staged single-skew layout)
directions.risk: Recent evidence says feed-path changes are dangerous; even a bounded skew retune can regress the near-hit if it reintroduces the same class of shared/LSU problems as round 1.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if DRAM / LSU pressure does not improve quickly or if the bounded stride retune loses the round-4 near-hit behavior.

notes: Round 5/5 diagnosis prepared from near-hit run 20260419_100457_bf16_gemm_v1_6dd39ad. Evidence hierarchy: accepted base 16a98f7 remains nominally best at 37.285807 ms; round 1 two-level B staging regressed badly; round 2 phased 64x384 micro-panels regressed badly; round 3 warp-specialized staging improved one symptom but stayed much slower; round 4 fixed-shape peeled hot kernel nearly matched the base at 37.373951 ms while raising tensor active and lowering barrier stall. Final-round ranking therefore treats the peeled hot path as the most credible basis and prioritizes complementary one-round changes over reopening the clearly failed rewrite families.
